title: Morphology and geology of the continental shelf and upper slope of southern Central Chile (33°S–43°S)
abstract: The geologic setting of the southern Central Chilean continental shelf (33°S–43°S) is summarized using recently published geophysical, seismological, sedimentological and bio-geochemical data, together with unpublished reflection seismic profiles, swath bathymetry and biological observations. Shelf basins show highly variable thickness of Oligocene–Quaternary sedimentary units dissected by marine continuations of upper plate faults known from land. A paleo-accretionary complex forms the bulk of the continental margin, sandwiched between the present active accretionary prism and the continental crust.

location.description: Continental shelf and upper slope of southern Central Chile 33°–43°S; synthesis using published and unpublished data

seismic_lines.name: SO161-25
seismic_lines.notes: Unpublished N-S margin-parallel profile 36.75°S–40°S from SPOC SO161; presented here for first time

seismic_lines.name: RC2901 profiles (6)
seismic_lines.notes: R/V Conrad RC2901 reflection seismics, 32°–40°S; partly published in Bangs & Cande 1997, Díaz-Naveas 1999

seismic_lines.name: SO101-102 profiles (37)
seismic_lines.notes: R/V SONNE SO101-102, 21°–44°S; partly published in Flueh et al. 1998

seismic_lines.name: SO104 profiles (14)
seismic_lines.notes: R/V SONNE SO104, 20°–32°S; partly published in von Huene et al. 1997

seismic_lines.name: SO161 profiles (35)
seismic_lines.notes: R/V SONNE SO161 SPOC, 23°–38°S; partly published in Ranero et al. 2006, Contreras-Reyes et al. 2008

seismic_lines.name: SO181 profiles (4)
seismic_lines.notes: R/V SONNE SO181 TIPTEQ, 31°–47°S; partly published in Contreras-Reyes et al. 2008/2010

seismic_lines.name: VG02 profiles (18)
seismic_lines.notes: R/V Vidal Gormaz VG02, 31°–34°S; published in Contardo et al. 2008

seismic_lines.name: VG06 profiles
seismic_lines.notes: R/V Vidal Gormaz VG06, 32.5°–37°S; published in Contardo et al. 2008

data.name: Published and Unpublished MCS Profiles — Southern Central Chile Shelf 33°–43°S
data.description: Compilation of published and previously unpublished MCS reflection profiles of the continental shelf (33°–43°S); maps shelf basin architecture with Oligocene–Quaternary sediments and upper plate faults. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

data.name: Swath Bathymetry — Southern Central Chile Shelf 33°–43°S
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry for the shelf and upper slope of southern Central Chile; compilation from SPOC, TIPTEQ and other cruises. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

_data_note: IJES synthesis paper; uses published and unpublished GEOMAR MCS data. Published data accessible via MGDS.

processing.summary: Synthesis of published and unpublished MCS reflection profiles, swath bathymetry and geophysical data for the southern Central Chilean continental shelf (33°–43°S). The compilation maps shelf basin stratigraphy (Oligocene–Quaternary sedimentary units), identifies marine continuations of upper-plate faults from land, and reveals a paleo-accretionary complex sandwiched between the modern accretionary prism and continental crust.
workflow: 1. Data compilation from 12 cruises (SO101-102, SO103, SO104, SO156, SO161, SO181, SO210, JC23, RC2901, VG02, VG06, M67, MV1004)
2. Swath bathymetry processing: navigation check, interpolation of missing values, water depth calculation by ray tracing, artefact removal
3. Digital elevation models (DEMs) at 200 m grid point density
4. Merging with Zapata (2001) shelf bathymetry (800 m grid, 0–400 m depth, low weighting factor)
5. Manual removal of interpolation artefacts at grid boundaries
6. Integration with SRTM land topography (Farr et al. 2007)
7. Sediment echosounder (PARASOUND) data interpretation for sediment thickness and facies
8. Seismic reflection profile interpretation for shelf basin stratigraphy (Oligocene–Quaternary)
9. Mapping of marine continuations of upper-plate faults
11. Synthesis with published geological, seismological, sedimentological and bio-geochemical data
processing.notes: Includes previously unpublished MCS reflection line SO161-25 (N-S, 36.75°S–40°S) and sediment echosounder data. MB-Systems (Caress et al. 1996) confirmed for bathymetry processing.

analysis_notes: PDF confirmed correct (IJES 2014, Völker et al.). Corrected: authors (4→10), vessel list (1→13 from Table 1), year_acquired (null→9 years), source_type (expanded), seismic_lines (1 generic→8 named line sets), software (removed Kingdom Suite, added MB-Systems confirmed in text; kept GMT). Workflow expanded from paper methods. data[] entries retained as PANGAEA/MGDS synthesis compilations. bounding_box confirmed 33°–43°S. Open access status not stated in paper.
